commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes two root README contributor avatar entries from Markdown image links to HTML image tags with explicit 48x48 dimensions.

Reproducibility: yes. at source/proof level: current main uses Markdown for two 420x420 avatar assets, and the provided screenshots show the resulting contributor wall distortion. I did not run a live GitHub renderer locally in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can move the README avatar sizing behavior into the generator and regenerate the affected output.

target.title: fix(readme): enforce explicit width and height for distorted contributor
target.body: ## Summary It turns out 18-RAJAT and constansino are using GitHub's default SVG identicons. Standard Markdown combined with max-width: 100% was letting these specific SVG avatars go wild and render at full size, which completely broke the grid. Instead of hiding or removing these contributors, I just fixed their formatting in README.md by switching them from Markdown to explicit HTML img tags with width="48" and height="48" attributes. I already pushed the fix to my fork, so you can check it out and grab it from there. Enforcing the dimensions at the HTML level forces the browser to keep them at exactly 48x48px no matter what. This way, the guys stay on the wall where they belong, and the layout bug is gone for good! Problem: GitHub's default SVG identicons used by contributors 18-RAJAT and constansino were ignoring the grid layout. Because they were formatted in standard Markdown alongside a CSS max-width: 100% rule, these specific SVG avatars rendered at full size and completely broke the contributor grid layout. Solution: Enforce explicit image dimensions at the HTML level instead of relying on standard Markdown. This forces the browser to restrict the avatars to a fixed size, resolving the layout bug while keeping the contributors visible on the wall. What changed: The formatting for the two specific contributors (18-RAJAT and constansino) in README.md was switched from standard Markdown image syntax to explicit HTML tags containing hardcoded width="48" and height="48" attributes. What did NOT change (scope boundary): No contributors were hidden or removed from the repository. The formatting for all other contributor avatars remains untouched, and no global CSS rules or external grid styles were modified. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix ## Linked Issue/PR - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Motivation This change is required to resolve a distinct visual regression on the repository's main landing page. Failure Mode: When a contributor uses a default GitHub SVG identicon, standard Markdown formatting combined with the repository's max-width: 100% CSS rule allows the browser to scale the SVG to its full, native dimensions rather than constraining it. User Pain / Maintainer Need: This behavior completely breaks the layout grid of the "clawtributors" section in the README.md, pushing other elements out of alignment and degrading the professional look of the project's front page. Product Goal: By explicitly defining the image dimensions at the HTML level (width="48" height="48"), we force proper browser boundaries on arbitrary SVG formats. This ensures a consistent, clean layout for all profile cards while keeping these contributors fully recognized on the wall. - ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) Behavior or issue addressed: GitHub default SVG identicons for specific contributors (18-RAJAT and constansino) were bypassing standard Markdown sizing constraint rules, rendering at full native size and breaking the alignment of the contributor grid layout. Real environment tested: GitHub Markdown Rendering Engine / Browser Preview (README.md). Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. Updated the image markdown structure for the affected profiles in README.md. 2. Pushed the changes to the remote branch on the fork. 3. Inspected the live repository comparison diff page to ensure the HTML tags were properly written and closed. Evidence after fix: ```html ... Observed result after fix: The browser explicitly overrides the SVG styling boundaries using the hardcoded HTML width and height attributes. The two problematic avatars are locked down to exactly 48x48px, restoring alignment consistency across the entire contributor wall. What was not tested: Automated visual regression test suites (the fix was visually verified via manual inspection of the Markdown output). Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Standard Markdown layout rules combined with the global CSS max-width: 100% property caused the unconstrained SVGs to stretch completely out of bounds. ## Root Cause (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. Root cause: Standard Markdown image syntax (![](url)) compiles into a clean HTML tag without native size attributes. When the browser renders an unconstrained SVG (like GitHub’s default identicons) inside a container governed by max-width: 100%, the image scales up to its native, un-rasterized asset dimensions rather than matching the layout grid of surrounding standard JPEG/PNG user avatars. Missing detection / guardrail: The contributor wall formatting relies purely on visual inspection and lacks automated layout validation or linting rules (e.g., a Markdown linter rule mandating explicit dimensions or a dedicated component wrapper) to flag raw SVG source paths before they are merged. Contributing context (if known): Standard custom avatars uploaded by users are pre-processed, scaled, and served by GitHub as fixed-dimension raster files, which naturally conform to the grid layout. Only default SVG identicons bypass this implicit behavior. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) Coverage level that should have caught this: [ ] Unit test [ ] Seam / integration test [ ] End-to-end test [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient Target test or file: README.md Scenario the test should lock in: Static code analysis or automated Markdown linting verifying that all contributor avatar image references enforce explicit HTML dimensions (width/height) instead of relying on raw Markdown syntax. Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: This is a purely static layout bug inside a documentation file; a custom markdown linter or CI layout verification tool would catch missing image attributes before rendering. Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A If no new test is added, why not: The repository does not currently use an automated layout test framework or visual regression pipeline for documentation elements. The fix was verified purely through visual inspection of the rendered page. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes The grid formatting on the "clawtributors" wall in README.md is restored. The avatars for 18-RAJAT and constansino now properly scale to a neat $48 \times 48\text{px}$ box matching the rest of the contributors, instead of expanding to break the row structure. ## Diagram (if applicable) Before: [Load README.md] -> [Browser parses SVG identicon without dimensions] -> [Grid breaks due to massive full-sized image] After: [Load README.md] -> [Browser reads explicit width/height tags] -> [Avatars stay confined at 48x48px grid box] ## Security Impact (required) -New permissions/capabilities? (No) -Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No) -New/changed network calls? (No) -Command/tool execution surface changed? (No) -Data access scope changed? (No) ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Runtime/container: Modern Web Browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari) / GitHub Markdown Engine Model/provider: N/A Integration/channel (if any): N/A Relevant config (redacted): N/A ### Steps 1. Navigate to the main repository page or view the raw README.md preview profile block. 2. Locate the contributor avatars for 18-RAJAT and constansino. 3. Inspect the DOM rendering behavior or look closely at the grid column alignments. ### Expected All contributor avatars display uniform dimensions ($48 \times 48\text{px}$), maintaining a completely symmetrical row and column layout on the wall.- ### Actual The two unconstrained default GitHub SVG identicons expand to their maximum native asset sizes, blowing up the container row layout and misaligning neighboring images. ## Evidence Before: Code snippet: [![constansino](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/65108260?v=4&s=48)](https://github.com/constansino) [![Rajat Joshi](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/78920780?v=4&s=48)](https://github.com/18-RAJAT After: Code snippet: ## Human Verification (required) Verified scenarios: Visually checked that the inline HTML tags override standard layout stretching inside the browser view engine.Edge cases checked: Resized the window viewport to ensure responsively scaled parent containers still properly force the $48 \times 48\text{px}$ fixed-dimension restriction on the explicit HTML targets.What you did not verify: Did not test behavior across text-only or legacy terminal-based Markdown viewers (e.g., Lynx). ## Review Conversations [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration -Backward compatible? (Yes) -Config/env changes? (No) -Migration needed? (No) ## Risks and Mitigations None. This is a completely risk-free documentation style adjustment that alters standard Markdown rendering to explicit inline HTML targeting elements on the frontend layout only.
target.author: aymanhaidry

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
